examples.title: Tree Traversals (Recursive)
examples.description: All three recursive traversal algorithms for binary trees
examples.code:
// PREORDER TRAVERSAL
PREORDER(node)
BEGIN
    IF node = NULL THEN
        RETURN
    ENDIF
    VISIT(node)              // Process root first
    PREORDER(node.left)      // Then left subtree
    PREORDER(node.right)     // Then right subtree
END

// INORDER TRAVERSAL
INORDER(node)
BEGIN
    IF node = NULL THEN
        RETURN
    ENDIF
    INORDER(node.left)       // Left subtree first
    VISIT(node)              // Then root
    INORDER(node.right)      // Then right subtree
END

// POSTORDER TRAVERSAL
POSTORDER(node)
BEGIN
    IF node = NULL THEN
        RETURN
    ENDIF
    POSTORDER(node.left)     // Left subtree
    POSTORDER(node.right)    // Right subtree
    VISIT(node)              // Root last
END
examples.explanation: Preorder visits root first (useful for copying trees), Inorder gives sorted output for BST, Postorder visits root last (useful for deletion)
examples.spaceComplexity: O(h) where h is height of tree

examples.title: Level Order Traversal
examples.description: Breadth-first traversal using queue data structure
examples.code:
LEVEL-ORDER(root)
BEGIN
    IF root = NULL THEN
        RETURN
    ENDIF
    
    CREATE queue Q
    ENQUEUE(Q, root)
    
    WHILE Q is not empty DO
        node = DEQUEUE(Q)
        VISIT(node)
        
        IF node.left != NULL THEN
            ENQUEUE(Q, node.left)
        ENDIF
        
        IF node.right != NULL THEN
            ENQUEUE(Q, node.right)
        ENDIF
    ENDWHILE
END
examples.explanation: Uses queue to process nodes level by level from left to right. Essential for breadth-first operations.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(w) where w is maximum width of tree, worst case O(n)

examples.title: BST Search Operation
examples.description: Implementation of search in Binary Search Tree
examples.explanation: Recursively compares target with nodes, going left for smaller values and right for larger. Returns node when found.
examples.timeComplexity: O(h) - O(log n) best/average, O(n) worst case
examples.spaceComplexity: O(h) for recursive, O(1) for iterative

examples.title: Binary Tree Creation and Traversal
examples.description: Complete C program demonstrating tree creation and all traversals
examples.explanation: Complete working example showing tree node structure and all three recursive traversals in C.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(h) where h is height (recursion stack)
